sections.title: Application
sections.description: Top-level application metadata. Sets the application name, version, and required engine version.

sections.title: Modules
sections.description: Modules are the building blocks of a workflow application. Each module represents a runtime service component (HTTP server, database connection, message broker, etc.).

requiredFields.name: name
requiredFields.description: Unique identifier for this module instance

requiredFields.name: type
requiredFields.description: Module type from the registry (e.g., http.server, database.workflow)

optionalFields.name: dependsOn
optionalFields.description: Module names this module depends on (controls init order)

sections.example:
modules:
  - name: api-server
    type: http.server
    config:
      address: ":8080"
relationships: Module names must be unique across all imported files

sections.title: Pipelines
sections.description: Pipelines define ordered sequences of steps to execute. Each pipeline is a named list of steps with optional flow control.
sections.example:
pipelines:
  process-order:
    steps:
      - type: step.transform
        config:
          template: '{{ .input }}'
relationships: Pipelines are referenced by module config and workflow handlers
Steps within a pipeline can reference other pipelines via step.pipeline

sections.title: Workflows
sections.description: Workflows define how external events trigger pipeline execution. Supports HTTP, messaging, state machine, and event-driven patterns.
sections.example:
workflows:
  http:
    server: api-server
    router: api-router
    routes:
      - path: /orders
        method: POST
        pipeline: process-order
relationships: HTTP workflows require a module of type http.server
Messaging workflows require a module of type messaging.broker

sections.title: Imports
sections.description: Imports allow splitting large workflow configs across multiple files. Imported files are merged into the root config at load time.

optionalFields.name: imports
optionalFields.description: Relative paths to partial config files to merge

relationships: Imported files are merged before validation
Name conflicts across imported files cause validation errors
